$schema_note: Each item: id, title, owner_class, code_actionable, detector (with signals[]), prompt. Detectors are stack-agnostic: each item lists multiple signal queries; the detector hits if ANY signal returns a non-empty result, unless `require: "all"` is set. Signal kinds: grep (patterns + paths), file_exists (paths), manual. `policy_source` below is a placeholder — the skill substitutes it from the operator's profile file (see SKILL.md Phase -1) at runtime.

items.title: Create and maintain an AI register for approved and proposed AI use cases
Define AI risk assessment and approval workflow for low, medium and high-risk use cases
Confirm approved AI tools and communicate prohibited use rules to staff
Develop role-based responsible AI training and awareness material
Add AI monitoring, audit logging, rollback and incident response requirements to operational procedures

done_requires: An audit logger / module exists AND is actually invoked from the AI orchestrator (not just defined)
Each audit record literally carries `model_id` (or equivalent), `prompt_version`, and `prompt_hash` (or sha256-of-prompt). The presence of `model` / `tokens` / `latency` alone is NOT sufficient.
An IR runbook + rollback procedure are documented (controls doc §5.3 / §5.4 or equivalents)
verdict_criteria.partial_when: Audit module exists and is wired, BUT one or more of `model_id`/`prompt_version`/`prompt_hash` is absent from the per-event payload, OR the IR/rollback runbooks are missing.
verdict_criteria.gap_when: No audit emitter wired into the AI orchestrator, OR the emitter only logs operational metrics (tokens/duration) with no per-event identity (no `model_id`/`prompt_version`).
verdict_criteria.critical_check: Before declaring Done, grep the audit-emitter file(s) for the literal strings `prompt_version`, `prompt_hash`, and a model-id field. If any of the three is absent → verdict is at most Partial.

items.title: Review alignment with privacy, access management, cloud security, backup, open source and human rights controls
Establish agent identity and cryptographic verification standards for external-facing AI agents
items.trigger_condition: any cross-boundary write or autonomous action

items.title: Define and document cross-boundary AI agent contracts, SLAs and commercial terms with partners
Create and maintain a canonical data dictionary for cross-boundary AI data exchanges
Implement data lineage tracking for derived and transformed data across partner networks
Conduct cross-boundary threat modelling including indirect prompt injection and supply chain risks

detector.note: Item 11 itself (the threat-model document) is often present; sub-items 11a (cross-tenant test) and 11b (cost rate-limit) are usually missing. Evaluate them independently.

applicability_check.on_zero_evidence_template: No tenant identifier found in code. If this is an internal single-workspace/single-customer service, item 11a does not apply as written. Replace with a scope-regression test for the relevant boundary (e.g. channel-scope for chat bots, role-scope for admin tools).

items.title: Assign autonomy levels to all approved AI use cases in the AI register

infer_when_miss.instructions: Before asking the operator, attempt to infer the autonomy level by reading: (1) the system prompt / instructions string (look for `SYSTEM_PROMPT`, `instructions:`, `prompt = '''...'''`, `system: ...` in agent/orchestrator source); (2) tool allowlists / denylists (look for `ALLOWED_*`, `READONLY_*`, denylist arrays, guardrails on write operations); (3) human-confirmation flows (UI buttons like Confirm/Edit/Cancel, `requires_approval`, `human_in_the_loop` flags). Heuristics: prompt says 'read-only' OR all tools are read commands → **Advisor**. Tools include writes but UI requires explicit user approval → **Assistant (human sign-off)**. Tools include writes that execute without per-action confirmation but inside hard-coded scope limits → **Autonomous (guardrailed)**. If none of the above can be determined with high confidence, FALL BACK to the manual_prompt below. Always cite the file:line(s) that supported the inference.

manual_prompt_fallback.question: Per your policy's autonomy-level section, which autonomy level applies to this service's AI use case? Choose: Advisor (read-only) / Assistant (human sign-off) / Autonomous (guardrailed) / Prohibited autonomous.

items.title: Establish agent sandbox or simulation environments for multi-agent system testing
Define continuous learning governance controls for all AI systems that adapt over time
Evaluate AI-assisted quality verification for designated high-risk output categories
Update incident response procedures to include cross-boundary agent incident coordination with partners
